portalItems.title: NSIsite
portalItems.snippet: The NSI site dataset is a unique collection of a vast range of detailed information. Based on a systematic grid, spaced at 5km intervals, the NSI data can be used in geostatisticical methods as well as a more general information source. Recent studies have included the use of this data in sophisticated erosion modelling. This data is essential for those trying to build up a detailed picture, for example, of chemical trends across England and Wales.

portalItems.title: NATMAPwrb2006
portalItems.snippet: NATMAPwrb2006 (National Soil Map - World Reference Base 2006 Version) is derived from the National Soil Map at 1:250,000 scale map of England and Wales, showing the locations of 113 distinct soil classes wherever they occur within the countries. The dominant soil series in each soil association has been classified to show the equivalent soil class in the World Reference Base classification system of 2006 as defined in World Soil Resources reports No. 103.

portalItems.title: SoilCatalogue_63k
portalItems.snippet: Location of the 1:63,000 soil maps

portalItems.title: NSImagnetic
portalItems.snippet: NSI Magnetic is a soil magnetic suseptibility dataset. Based on a systematic grid, spaced at 5 km intervals, the NSI data can be used in geostatisticical methods as well as a more general information source. Recent studies have included the use of this data in sophisticated erosion modelling. This data is essential for those trying to build up a detailed picture, for example, of chemical trends across England and Wales.

portalItems.title: HORIZONfundamentals
portalItems.snippet: Tabular data that can be used in conjunction with any of the NATMAP products except NATMAP soilscapes. Used in assessing or modelling the capability or vulnerability of land. HORIZON fundamentals includes detailed descriptions of the texture and structure of each soil horizon.

portalItems.title: SoilCatalogue_FarmSurveys
portalItems.snippet: Location of the farm survey soil maps

portalItems.title: NATMAPsubstratetexture
portalItems.snippet: NATMAPsubstratetexture is a substrate texture map derived from the 1:250,000 scale National Soil Map. It provides users the range of subsoil texture classes typical at a depth of 80 cm across England and Wales.

portalItems.title: Portal User Survey_form
portalItems.snippet: This welcome form is designed for new users to the soils portal to inform Cranfield about yourself and your interest in the data. Where possible, and particularly for Defra Group users, please use your institutional email to register your interest. Any queries, please contact landis@cranfield.ac.uk.

portalItems.title: SoilCatalogue_RabbitSurveys_sites
portalItems.snippet: Location of the Rabbit Survey squares soil maps

portalItems.title: NATMAPsubsoiltexture
portalItems.snippet: NATMAPsubsoiltexture is a subsoil texture map derived from the 1:250,000 scale National Soil Map. It provides users the range of subsoil texture classes typical at a depth of 40 cm across England and Wales.

portalItems.title: AUGERsite
portalItems.snippet: Site properties of auger bores collected during systematic soil survey

portalItems.title: NSIprofile
portalItems.snippet: The National Soil Inventory (NSI) profile data is a spatial, point dataset with 6127 points, located in a 5 km grid across England and Wales. NSI profile provides a very detailed description of the soil profile at the NSI point.

portalItems.title: SOILSERIES
portalItems.snippet: Tabular data that can be used in conjunction with any of the NATMAP products except NATMAP soilscapes. SOILSERIES provides basic information on the soil series, including the modern definition of the soil.

portalItems.title: NATMAPtopsoiltexture
portalItems.snippet: NATMAPtopsoiltexture is a topsoil texture map derived from the 1:250,000 scale National Soil Map. It provides users the range of topsoil texture classes typical across England and Wales.

portalItems.title: NATMAPassociations
portalItems.snippet: NATMAP Associations (version 3). List of soil series components of soil associations with proportions (%)

portalItems.title: NATMAPregions
portalItems.snippet: NATMAPregions defines the six regions used in the original National Soil Mapping Bulletin. For each region the National Soil Map was published along with an accompanying bulletin.

portalItems.title: NATMAP2000
portalItems.snippet: This product is useful for anyone wanting a general understanding of the soil series within a region, for those who are not too concerned with actual line work, but only require the overall breakdown of soils within their area.

portalItems.title: NSItexture
portalItems.snippet: National Soil Inventory (NSI) analytical results for particle size distribution

portalItems.title: NSItopsoil1
portalItems.snippet: The initial NSI topsoil analyses. NSI topsoil1 contains a comprehensive record of many of the important elemental abundances in the soils of the UK.

portalItems.title: SoilCatalogue_25k
portalItems.snippet: Location of the 1:25,000 soil maps

portalItems.title: NATMAPsoilscapes
portalItems.snippet: NATMAP soilscapes is a simplified version of the National Soil Map

portalItems.title: NSItopsoil2
portalItems.snippet: NSI topsoil analyses (resample). The NSI dataset is a unique collection of a vast range of detailed information. Based on a systematic grid, spaced at 5km intervals, the NSI data can be used in geostatisticical methods as well as a more general information source. Recent studies have included the use of this data in sophisticated erosion modelling. This data is essential for those trying to build up a detailed picture, for example, of chemical trends across England and Wales.

portalItems.title: AUGERprofile
portalItems.snippet: Profile horizon properties of auger bores collected during systematic soil survey

portalItems.title: NSIfeatures
portalItems.snippet: The NSI features dataset is a unique collection of a vast range of detailed information. Based on a systematic grid, spaced at 5 km intervals, the NSI data can be used in geostatisticical methods as well as a more general information source. Recent studies have included the use of this data in sophisticated erosion modelling. This data is essential for those trying to build up a detailed picture, for example, of chemical trends across England and Wales.

portalItems.title: NATMAPavailablewater
portalItems.snippet: The Crop Available Water map is derived from the 1:250,000 scale National Soil Map. It shows the average crop-adjusted available water capacity of the soils across England and Wales.

portalItems.title: NationalSoilMap
portalItems.snippet: The National Soil Map is a vector dataset derived from the National Soil Map for England and Wales and is the product of sixty years of soil survey work in England and Wales.

portalItems.title: HORIZONhydraulics
portalItems.snippet: Tabular data that can be used in conjunction with any of the NATMAP products except NATMAP soilscapes. Used in assessing or modelling the capability or vulnerability of land. HORIZON hydraulics provides a range of hydraulic properties, for example water content, for each soil series-layer (soil horizon).

portalItems.title: NATMAPcarbon
portalItems.snippet: NATMAPcarbon is a soil organic carbon map derived from the 1:250,000 scale National Soil Map and the 1:100,000 scale Corine land cover 2000 map. It provides users with a summary of the stock of carbon in the soils across England and Wales at three layer depths 0-30 cm, 30-100 cm and 100-150 cm.

portalItems.title: SoilCatalogue_50k
portalItems.snippet: Location of the 1:50,000 soil maps. The outlines of the maps were extracted from each of the published maps, then checked and validated by NSRI staff.

portalItems.title: NATMAPlegend
portalItems.snippet: NATMAP Legend (version 3). Text descriptions of geology, soil and site characteristics and of cropping and land use for each map unit of the National Soil Map.

portalItems.title: SoilCatalogue_100k
portalItems.snippet: Location of the 1:100,000 soil maps

portalItems.title: NATMAP1000
portalItems.snippet: This product is useful for anyone wanting a general understanding of the soil series within a region, for those who are not too concerned with actual line work, but only require the overall breakdown of soils within their area.

portalItems.title: NATMAP5000
portalItems.snippet: This product is useful for anyone wanting a general understanding of the soil series within a region, for those who are not too concerned with actual line work, but only require the overall breakdown of soils within their area.

portalItems.title: NSI Explorer
portalItems.snippet:
App showing data from the National Soil Inventory, gridded soil monitoring datasets, for the original national survey and the follow up resurvey. The NSI Explorer app is one of a series of web experience apps developed in ArcGIS Online to exemplify and demonstrate some of the key datasets provided in the LandIS Portal. The NSI Explorer showcases a key dataset in the portal, the National Soil Inventory, NSI.

The NSI site dataset is a unique collection of a vast range of detailed information. Based on a systematic grid, spaced at 5km intervals, the NSI data can be used in geostatisticical methods as well as a more general information source. Recent studies have included the use of this data in sophisticated erosion modelling. This data is essential for those trying to build up a detailed picture, for example, of chemical trends across England and Wales.

portalItems.title: Detailed Mapping Explorer
portalItems.snippet: This is a web map designed to support the Detailed Mapping Explorer app - one of a series of web experience apps developed in ArcGIS Online to exemplify and demonstrate some of the key datasets provided in the LandIS Portal. The Detailed Mapping Explorer shows the range of detailed mapping conducted across England and Wales, highlighting the bounding box of each map, shown as an outline. Mapping is provided at a range of detailed scales.

portalItems.title: Detailed Mapping Explorer
portalItems.snippet: App showing the outline of all the detailed soil maps held in LandIS. The Detailed Mapping Explorer app is one of a series of web experience apps developed in ArcGIS Online to exemplify and demonstrate some of the key datasets provided in the LandIS Portal. The Detailed Mapping Explorer shows the range of detailed mapping conducted across England and Wales, highlighting the bounding box of each map, shown as an outline. Mapping is provided at a range of detailed scales.

portalItems.title: NSI Explorer
portalItems.snippet: This is a web map designed to support the NSI Explorer app - one of a series of web experience apps developed in ArcGIS Online to exemplify and demonstrate some of the key datasets provided in the LandIS Portal. The NSI Explorer showcases a key dataset in the portal, the National Soil Inventory, NSI.

portalItems.title: LandIS Portal
portalItems.snippet: The Land Information System (LandIS) Open Soils Data Portal

portalItems.title: Natmap_Soilscapes Explorer
portalItems.snippet: App showing data from both the national soil map and the Soilscapes map, two of the most important maps held in LandIS. The Natmap Explorer app is one of a series of web experience apps developed in ArcGIS Online to exemplify and demonstrate some of the key datasets provided in the LandIS Portal. The Natmap Explorer showcases two key datasets in the portal, the national soil map, Natmap, and the Natmap Soilscapes data.

portalItems.title: NationalSoilMap
portalItems.snippet: Style file for the national soil map with all symbology

portalItems.title: USER STORY 104394 - Hub Site Splash Screen Test
NatMap Explorer
portalItems.snippet: This is a web map designed to support the Natmap Explorer app - one of a series of web experience apps developed in ArcGIS Online to exemplify and demonstrate some of the key datasets provided in the LandIS Portal. The Natmap Explorer showcases two key datasets in the portal, the national soil map, Natmap, and the Natmap Soilscapes data.
